name: Maui Burgers
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix the beef, garlic, scallions, ginger, and soy sauce together. Form into 4 patties that are about 3/4 inch thick.
2. Grill or broil the patties 4 inches from the heat source for 7 minutes until medium-rare, turning once.
3. While grilling the burgers, brush 4 slices of fresh or canned pineapple with Worcestershire sauce.
4. Grill the pineapple slices until heated through and seared with grill marks, about 6 minutes (2 minutes for canned pineapple).
5. Serve each patty open-face on a 1 oz toasted slice of French bread, topped with grilled pineapple slice, and garnished with 1 tbsp minced green bell pepper.
Joslin Food Exchange per serving: 2 MEDIUM-FAT MEAT EXCHANGES + 1 1/2 BREAD/STARCH EXHANGES
CAL: 282; PRO: 20g; CAR: 23g; FAT: 13g (CAL: FROM FAT 41%) FIB: TRACE CHO: 53mg; SODIUM: 401mg; POTASSIUM: 307mg
Source: Joslin Diabetes Gourmet Cookbook
